profile_purpose: Inform article writing so that content resonates with fleet and operations managers at mid to large logistics and courier companies and drives engagement and conversion.
who_they_are: Fleet managers, heads of logistics, and operations managers at mid to large UK courier, last-mile delivery, and haulage operations. Managing between 30 and 500 vehicles across depots or regions. Under constant pressure to hit delivery windows, control costs, and maintain compliance. Accountable to senior leadership for fleet KPIs.
what_they_care_about: On-time delivery performance across the entire fleet
Real-time vehicle location and route adherence
Driver behaviour — speeding, harsh braking, idle time
Fuel efficiency across a high-mileage fleet
Vehicle security — cargo theft is a real and growing risk
Reducing accident rates and associated insurance costs
core_pains_to_address: Cargo theft — vehicles and loads targeted in transit or at rest stops
No real-time visibility when a driver goes off route or misses a window
High insurance premiums driven by fleet size and accident history
Driver hours violations creating compliance and legal exposure
Fuel costs that are difficult to control across a large dispersed fleet
Incidents with no footage — liability disputes with no evidence
beliefs_to_align_with: You cannot manage what you cannot see — real-time visibility is non-negotiable
Driver behaviour data is the fastest lever for reducing fuel and insurance costs
Cargo security is as important as vehicle security at this scale
Compliance is not optional — the cost of getting it wrong is too high
objections_to_pre-empt: We operate across multiple depots — can this scale and centralise reporting?
How does this integrate with our TMS or existing fleet management platform?
What is the ROI timeline on a fleet this size?
How quickly can incidents be flagged and acted on in real time?

use: Operational scale language — depots, routes, delivery windows, utilisation rates
Compliance framing — tachographs, driver hours, duty of care, DVSA
Cost reduction metrics — fuel per mile, accident frequency rate, insurance premium per vehicle
Cargo and asset security framing alongside vehicle security
avoid: Small fleet or owner-operator language — this reader manages scale
Generic tracking language that does not acknowledge logistics complexity
Anything that sounds like a consumer product
Overpromising on integration without understanding their existing stack

conversion_goal: Reader sees Traknova as capable of operating at their scale and complexity, and requests a demo or structured conversation about fleet-wide deployment.
